Notice Of Motion And Motion To Send A Bellaire Notice To The Putative Class Members
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Carlos Jato, Esq. 415-606-3160 for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. Plaintiff requests for a deadline. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF VANESSA BUSTOS' Motion To Send A "Bellaire" Notice To The Putative Class Members.

Plaintiff's motion for an order requiring Defendants to send out the attached opt-out notice to all individuals whom Coffee Meets Bagel designated as independent contractors or employee consultants since May 6, 2016, is granted. A representative plaintiff's access to the contact information of other potential class members is "is within the proper scope of discovery, an essential first step to prosecution of any representative action." (Williams v. Superior Court (2017) 3 Cal.5th 531, 544). Although there are privacy considerations with distributing the contact information of current and former employees, those concerns are properly addressed by way of an opt-out notice. (Belaire-West Landscape, Inc. v. Superior Court (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 554, 562 ("Requiring current and former employees to object to disclosure of their identities and contact information presents no serious invasion of their privacy interests").)

In addition, Defendants must submit the contact information for the individuals to whom the bell air notice will be mailed to the administrator by 10/1/19.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
